description: All tests must pass before finishing work. Run `COVERAGE=1 bundle exec rspec` (and any relevant linting) before handoff or commit. Any failures — whether caused by your changes or pre-existing — must be fixed. Fix all warnings and errors in tests, build output, type checks, and runtime logs. Never silence or defer without a documented exception.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: Every `rescue` must call `ErrorReporter.report(exception)` OR log explicitly — never swallow silently. Sentry: StandardError subclasses, provider/webhook/job failures, data corruption. Log only: validation, auth, concurrency retries, expected record-not-found. Never: `rescue => e; nil` or `rescue => e; return false`. Full examples: `docs/roles/IMPLEMENTER.md`.
consequence: Data loss, billing errors, or irrecoverable corruption

description: Never execute raw SQL directly. Prefer ActiveRecord query APIs first. If inadequate, prefer Arel over raw SQL strings.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: Always use `policy_scope` for all scoped record lookups in controllers. Never inline AR scoping to restrict record visibility.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: Prefer correctness over speed. If a code change breaks existing specs, investigate why before adjusting anything. Do not weaken a spec to preserve a green run.
consequence: Hidden regressions, broken CI signal, false confidence in test suite

description: Test sync must use Capybara/RSpec primitives only — no `sleep`, no custom polling loops (`Timeout.timeout + loop`), no `wait:` tuning in `spec/**`. Use `have_css`, `have_text`, `have_current_path`, and shared semantic helpers that delegate to those primitives.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: For this Rails repo, never use generic test-runner helpers such as `runTests` or `runTest`. Use targeted `bundle exec rspec <specific_file>` directly in the devcontainer terminal instead so verification is authoritative and output stays small.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: Keep MCP server definitions canonical and synchronized. Source of truth: `docs/mcp/servers.yml`. Regenerate derived files with `ruby script/sync_mcp_configs.rb`. Do not hand-edit generated `mcp-servers` blocks in `.github/agents/playwright-test-*.agent.md` or `.mcp.json`.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: System specs exercise browser interactions only — `visit`, `click_button`, `fill_in`, `have_text`. Never use `page.driver.post/delete` or RSpec `post`/`delete` helpers in system specs. API contracts (including Stimulus AJAX) belong in request specs (`type: :request`). See `docs/roles/TESTER.md`.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: Never checkout, change, or merge into the `main` branch. All work happens on feature branches. Agent worktree branches merge into the feature branch, never into `main`. `main` is the production branch — only the user or CI merges into it.
consequence: Production outage, deployment failure, or degraded service

description: New agent rules and guardrails must be saved to the MCP server. Add rules as numbered items in `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (universal) or the appropriate `docs/roles/*.md` file (role-specific). Rebuild the harness DB after changes: `ruby script/build_harness_db.rb`. The harness MCP server is the canonical rule store — do not rely on file-based auto-memory for rules that belong in the project.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: Follow the ticket lifecycle in `docs/runbooks/agent-workflow.md`. States: orch researches → dispatches imp (In Progress) → imp moves to In Review → rev passes (merge + Done) or returns to orch (In Progress). Never skip states; comment at every transition. For tickets with multi-approach tradeoffs: add `needs-planning`, move to Backlog, and wait for user approval before dispatch. After all subtasks' worktrees merge to branch: `COVERAGE=1 bundle exec rspec`; failures → log to ticket + return to In Progress.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: Never skip, hide, or remove failing tests to force a green run. A breaking spec may be exposing a previously hidden bug. Fix the root cause in the code being tested or the spec's incorrect assumptions. If an agent-written spec fails, do NOT delete it. Debug the failure — check the page object methods, DOM selectors, auth setup, and factory traits against the actual code. Only remove a spec if it is truly a duplicate or tests behavior that no longer exists. Rewrite incorrect assertions, don't delete the spec.
consequence: Silent failures, hidden bugs, undetected errors in production

description: Always read and update the harness work log. You must read current context at the start of any work — latest resume note, open TODOs, recent done in `agent_harness.work_log` (auto-printed by the SessionStart hook, or `script/session_context.sh read`). Before finishing/handing off you must write back: `log`/`done` completed items, `add-todo` new work, `set-resume` a fresh handoff note. The harness work log — not chat scrollback or ad-hoc docs — is the canonical home for work-to-do / done / resume.
consequence: Broken invariants cause subtle bugs, CI failures, or production incidents

description: Keep the shared `global` rule tier identical across apps. These Universal Rules seed the cross-app `app='global'` tier of the harness DB, which every app reads. They must stay byte-identical in every app repo (`whatchapizza`/pamm, `kyra_api`, …). When you add or change a universal rule, mirror the exact same change into every other app's `.github/copilot-instructions.md` and rebuild (`HARNESS_APP=<app> ruby script/build_harness_db.rb`) — the global tier is shared and the last app to build wins, so divergence silently clobbers it. App-specific rules belong in `docs/roles/*.md` (the per-app tier), never here.
consequence: Silent failures, hidden bugs, undetected errors in production
